01
Sales Analytics
Sales Performance &
Decision Intelligence
+
The Problem

The company had access to large volumes of ERP sales data but lacked any structured visibility into performance. Decisions were being made on intuition rather than evidence, with no defined KPIs and no single source of truth for leadership.

My Approach

Designed a full Power BI solution from the ground up, starting with data architecture and ending with executive-ready visualizations aligned to real business goals.

Star Schema ERP Integration DAX Measures KPI Definition Stakeholder Alignment
What I Built
  • Revenue tracking, monthly & year-over-year
  • Sales team individual performance ranking
  • Customer behavior & lifecycle analysis
  • Average ticket evolution over time
Business Impact
  • Accelerated decision-making speed across leadership
  • Identified underperforming segments and concentration risks
  • Enabled a fully data-driven sales strategy
  • Surfaced high-value customers for targeted action
02
Customer Intelligence
Customer Segmentation
— RFV Model
+
The Problem

The company treated all customers identically, no segmentation, no prioritization, no differentiated strategy. High-value clients received the same attention as dormant accounts, wasting commercial effort at scale.

My Approach

Rebuilt the RFV (Recency, Frequency, Value) model from scratch using real behavioral data. Created a scoring system that classifies 100% of customers into actionable strategic segments.

RFV Scoring Behavioral Segmentation DAX Customer Lifecycle
Key Insights
  • Clear identification of VIP customers by revenue contribution
  • Early detection of at-risk and churning clients
  • Prioritized commercial team focus by segment value
Business Impact
  • More efficient and targeted sales outreach
  • Better allocation of commercial effort by value tier
  • Increased revenue potential through strategic prioritization
03
Retention Analytics
Retention & Cohort
Analysis
+
The Problem

The company had no visibility into retention behavior. Leadership couldn't answer basic questions: Are customers coming back? When do they churn? Which acquisition cohorts are healthiest?

My Approach

Built a cohort model anchored to customer acquisition date, then designed a retention matrix with a visual heatmap that makes performance patterns immediately readable, even for non-technical stakeholders.

Cohort Analysis Retention Matrix Heatmap Viz Churn Detection
Key Insights
  • Identified specific time windows with highest churn risk
  • Revealed onboarding inefficiencies in early cohorts
  • Mapped weak retention periods to commercial events
Business Impact
  • Enabled proactive retention intervention strategy
  • Better understanding of the full customer lifecycle
  • Measurable reduction in churn risk exposure
04
Revenue Strategy
Revenue Concentration
— ABC / Pareto Analysis
+
The Problem

Revenue distribution was opaque. No one knew which clients or products drove the majority of results, or how exposed the business was to losing a handful of key accounts.

My Approach

Built an ABC classification model ranking customers and products by revenue contribution, then applied the Pareto principle to surface concentration risks and strategic opportunities in one clear view.

ABC Classification Pareto Analysis Revenue Attribution Risk Assessment
Key Insights
  • Confirmed revenue is heavily concentrated in a small client tier
  • Identified hidden dependency risks in the portfolio
  • Highlighted underserved growth opportunities in Class B/C
Business Impact
  • Strategic focus redirected to key accounts
  • Improved risk management and revenue resilience
  • Smarter, evidence-based portfolio decisions

Value Proposition
What Makes
Me Different
01
Business Mindset First

Every analytical decision is anchored in real business outcomes — not technical showcasing. I ask "what decision does this enable?" before writing a single DAX measure.

02
Real-World Sales Experience

I've worked inside sales environments, which means I understand the pressures, the data blind spots, and the questions that actually keep commercial leaders up at night.

03
Impact Over Aesthetics

I don't build dashboards to impress — I build them to drive action. Every visual earns its place by making a decision faster, clearer, or better informed.

04
End-to-End BI Ownership

From raw ERP data to executive presentation — I own the full stack. No handoffs, no gaps, no "that's not my part." One person accountable for the entire solution.
